checks.name: Concurrency: readyCh close happens-before configMu write ordering
checks.evidence: registry.go:121-138 (both services). configMu.Lock brackets write, readyOnce.Do(close(readyCh)) runs after Unlock; readers RLock, so happens-before edge holds.

checks.name: time.After allocation under high call rate
checks.evidence: registry.go:48 (both services). Hot path short-circuits via closed channel in select; timer only allocated during cold-start ≤60s window.

checks.name: GetServiceConfig nil-check returns wrong sentinel after PublishSnapshot(nil, ...)
checks.evidence: registry.go:59-61 returns ErrNotReady when serviceConfig is nil even after readyCh closed; PublishSnapshot(nil,...) at registry.go:127 can produce this state. ErrNotReady misrepresents the situation.

checks.name: configuration.Init legacy REST path is dead code
checks.evidence: registry.go:89-111 (both services). grep -rn 'configuration.Init' returns zero hits. Still log.Fatalf-s — reintroduction risk.

checks.name: GetTenantConfigs silently swallows ErrNotReady
checks.evidence: registry.go:65-74 (atlas-channel only). _ = waitReady() drops error; no production caller exists today. Either change signature or delete.

checks.name: Callers distinguish ErrTenantNotConfigured from ErrNotReady
checks.evidence: services/atlas-login/atlas.com/login/socket/handler/accept_tos.go:45-49 and services/atlas-login/atlas.com/login/kafka/consumer/account/session/consumer.go:88-92 both log 'Unable to find server configuration' for either sentinel. Observability regression vs prior fatal.

checks.name: session.NewTimeout swallows error into 1-hour default without logging
checks.evidence: services/atlas-login/atlas.com/login/session/task.go:22-40 and services/atlas-channel/atlas.com/channel/session/task.go:22-40. err != nil branch sets to=3600000 with no log.

checks.name: Per-process projection group id leaves orphan Kafka groups
checks.evidence: services/atlas-channel/atlas.com/channel/main.go:235 and services/atlas-login/atlas.com/login/main.go:106. New uuid per restart; eventually GC'd by Kafka offsets.retention.minutes default 7d.

checks.name: Projection group id inherits env override via consumergroup.Resolve prefix
checks.evidence: main.go:62 (login) / main.go:150 (channel): consumerGroupId = consumergroup.Resolve(...); projection id Sprintf prepends this resolved value.

checks.name: Tests reset package state
checks.evidence: registry_test.go (both services). No t.Cleanup, no TestMain, no test-only reset export; PublishSnapshot mutates package vars and closes readyCh permanently for the test binary.

checks.name: PublishSnapshot value-copy doc claim is accurate (deep vs shallow)
checks.evidence: registry.go:121-134 doc says 'value-copy so caller's projection State can mutate independently'. c := *svc is shallow — Tasks/Tenants slice headers still share backing arrays.

checks.name: waitReady accepts a context.Context for cancellation
checks.evidence: registry.go:44-51 (both services). No ctx parameter; Kafka/handler caller cannot cancel a 60s wait, risking partition rebalance.

checks.name: go build ./... clean
checks.evidence: Zero output from go build ./... in both services.

blocking: FU-03: GetServiceConfig returns ErrNotReady for the cleared-after-ready case (wrong sentinel)
FU-04: configuration.Init is dead code, still log.Fatalf-s, no callers
FU-05: GetTenantConfigs silently swallows ErrNotReady because signature has no error return
FU-06: accept_tos.go:45 and kafka/consumer/account/session/consumer.go:88 do not distinguish ErrTenantNotConfigured from ErrNotReady
FU-07: session.NewTimeout falls back to 1h default with no log on configuration error
FU-10: registry_test.go has no test isolation; pollutes package state for any second test
nonBlocking: FU-02: time.After allocation pattern (not actually hot-path; comment-worthy)
FU-08: per-process projection group id leaves orphan Kafka consumer groups on restart
FU-11: PublishSnapshot doc claims value-copy but performs shallow copy
FU-12: waitReady ignores context.Context; risks Kafka partition rebalance on stall
